title: Reverse-auditor output
description: Constrains the reverse-auditor judge's single-emission JSON output. A distinct top-level `coverage_state` field is carried on every emission: `covered` (the judge adjudicated from the inlined packet) or `insufficient-evidence` (the inlined packet was inadequate to adjudicate — a visible non-verdict, NOT silence). The `omission_claim` slot disjoins between Shape A (object — one grounded omission claim) and Shape B (null — explicit silence or the abstention companion). Three caller-visible states: {coverage_state:covered, omission_claim:null} = covered silence; {coverage_state:covered, omission_claim:{…}} = grounded omission; {coverage_state:insufficient-evidence, omission_claim:null, abstention_reason, insufficient_evidence_refs[]} = abstention. `additionalProperties: false` at root AND inside `omission_claim` mechanically rejects legacy/drifted fields (`verdict`, `verdict_source`, `claim`, `no-omission`) even when the prompt already forbids them. Per the json-schema-codex convention the disjunct uses anyOf+type (never oneOf). Template-version identity is enforced post-parse in scripts/audit-artifact.sh — this schema only pins the 12-char hex shape.

anyOf.title: Shape B — explicit silence (or abstention companion)
Shape A — one grounded omission claim
